things.name: Tinganes
things.description: One of Europe's oldest continuously used governmental sites; turf-roofed red and black wooden buildings on a rocky peninsula have housed Faroese government since Viking times

things.name: Torshavn Old Town (Gongin)
things.description: Charming historic quarter with grass-roofed wooden houses, cobbled lanes and the authentic character of a North Atlantic Viking settlement

things.name: National Museum of the Faroe Islands
things.description: Comprehensive museum covering Faroese natural history, Viking heritage, traditional dress, boats and the unique cultural identity of the archipelago

things.name: Torshavn Cathedral
things.description: Simple but atmospheric wooden cathedral from 1788, one of the oldest churches in the Faroe Islands with a striking interior and peaceful graveyard

things.name: Skansin Fortress
things.description: Old fort at the harbour entrance built in 1580 to protect the city from pirates; British lighthouse keepers based here in WWII; now a historic park

things.name: Listasavn Foroya (National Art Gallery)
things.description: The Faroe Islands' principal art gallery with a strong collection of Faroese visual art from the 19th century to the present in a striking modern building

things.name: Torshavn Harbour
things.description: Working fishing harbour at the heart of the capital with colourful fishing boats, seafood restaurants and views across the fjord to the neighbouring islands

things.name: Kirkjubour Village
things.description: Ancient bishopric seat 10km south of Torshavn with the 13th-century Magnus Cathedral ruins, the 11th-century St. Olav's Church and a farm museum

things.name: Nordic House Torshavn
things.description: Cultural centre designed by Finnish architect Alvar Aalto hosting art exhibitions, concerts and events celebrating Nordic and Faroese culture

things.name: Torsvollur National Stadium
things.description: The Faroe Islands' national stadium, home ground for Vikingur Reykjavik's Faroese counterpart and international matches, with a capacity of 6,000
